Please add new ideas as well.

* date picker (useful for ecology and meteorology, https://github.com/charliekassel/vuejs-datepicker)
 xref https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/6223
* location picker (lat/long (+altitude?) or graphical: https://www.npmjs.com/package/vue-location-picker)

          </valid>

quirks","user":{"login":"astrovsky01","id":40246333,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQwMjQ2MzMz","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/40246333?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/astrovsky01","html_url":"https://github.com/astrovsky01","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/astrovsky01/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/astrovsky01/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/astrovsky01/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/astrovsky01/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/astrovsky01/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/astrovsky01/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/astrovsky01/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/astrovsky01/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/astrovsky01/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[{"id":180024669,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwxODAwMjQ2Njk=","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/labels/kind/bug","name":"kind/bug","color":"84b6eb","default":false,"description":null},{"id":241204064,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwyNDEyMDQwNjQ=","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/labels/area/UI-UX","name":"area/UI-UX","color":"006b75","default":false,"description":null},{"id":242034312,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwyNDIwMzQzMTI=","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/labels/feature-request","name":"feature-request","color":"fef2c0","default":false,"description":null},{"id":2418032213,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwyNDE4MDMyMjEz","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/labels/paper-cut","name":"paper-cut","color":"c2e0c6","default":false,"description":""}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":3,"created_at":"2021-09-29T19:53:13Z","updated_at":"2022-04-27T20:56:49Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"**Describe the bug**
Unlike the toolbox search bar, the dataset and history search bars only perform a search after pressing the enter key, and will only clear a search after pressing the escape key or 'x'. 

Also, not sure if it is intended or not, but double quotes work for the search parameters, but not single quotes.

**Galaxy Version and/or server at which you observed the bug**
21.09 and 21.05 (both org and eu)

**To Reproduce**
1. Type search query in history search bar
2. wait for search to be performed without pressing key
3. press enter to perform search, then delete search parameter and wait for search to be updated
4. perform advanced parameter search identically with single and double quotes

**Expected behavior**
Search updates on text update. Search succeeds with both single and double quotes

A clear and concise description of what the bug is.

When using the uploader, clicking outside the modal will close it. With file upload, this is fine, but with rule-based uploading, this causes all progress made and rules set to be lost. Clicking away and reopening this will return the user to the pasted data, and require the user to 'build' and set the rules again. This should only close the modal, and not undo the rules, which should be cancelled via the 'cancel' button on the modal itself, otherwise someone could have many rules set, accidentally click away, and then have all of that work undone.

{"action":"created","comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/pulls/comments/785219900","pull_request_review_id":853451360,"id":785219900,"node_id":"PRRC_kwDOAdw-Nc4uzYE8","diff_hunk":"@@ -0,0 +1,70 @@
+<tool id="CONVERTER_uncompressed_to_gz" name="Compress file(s) using gzip" version="0.1.0">
+    <requirements>
+    </requirements>
+    <command detect_errors="exit_code"><![CDATA[
+        #if $files.type=="individual":
+            gzip -c ${files.input} > output_file.gz
+            #set $type = "${files.input.extension}.gz"
+        #else:
+            mkdir outdir &&
+            #for $i,$file in enumerate($files.input):
+                gzip -c ${file} > outdir/${file.element_identifier}.gz &&
+            #end for
+            ls outdir
+        #end if -->
+
+    ]]></command>
+    <inputs>
+        <conditional name="files">
+            <param name="type" type="select" label="Compress individual file or a collection?">
+                <option value="individual">Individual file</option>
+                <option value="collection">Collection</option>
+            </param>
+            <when value="individual">
+                <param name="input" type="data" format="data" label="Select data to compress"/>
+            </when>
+            <when value="collection">
+                    <param name="input" type="data_collection" collection_type="list" label="Select data to compress"/>
+            </when>
+        </conditional>
+    </inputs>

the bug** Datasets that have tags only sometimes show their tags when not expanded. The only way to get the dataset to always show the tag is to expand it and select the tag editor. Otherwise, as analyses are run with the datasets, the tags disappear from the general history until full page reload. **Galaxy Version and/or server at which you observed the bug** 21.09, 21.05, org and eu **To Reproduce** Steps to reproduce the behavior: 1. Upload a dataset 2. give dataset propagating tags 3. run tools on dataset 4. watch tags disappear on original dataset **Expected behavior** Dataset tags should always be visible **Screenshots** **Additional context** Add any other context about the problem here. Tags are also not visible on rules-based uploaded data until a page
